title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A day trip to Cape Cod on Memorial Day weekend costs approximately $162.75, including gas, beach parking, and meals.
A couple's getaway to Cape Cod from Friday to Monday totals around $1,867.55, covering lodging, meals, and entertainment.
A family vacation for four at Cape Cod for three nights is estimated at $2,331.35, including hotel stay, meals, and activities.

title: 5 arrested in brawl at Dorchester courthouse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Five individuals arrested following a brawl at Dorchester Division of Boston Municipal Court on Wednesday morning.
Suspects include four adults and one 17-year-old; all face charges of affray and disrupting court proceedings.
Three suspects pleaded not guilty and are held on $1,000 bail; a fourth's arraignment is pending.

title: Mass. man pleads guilty to prostitution and fraud charges over strip club activities, COVID loan
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Kenneth Denning, 69, from Holland, Mass., pleaded guilty to promoting prostitution at his strip club in Tolland, Connecticut.
Denning faces charges of conspiracy to defraud the IRS, commit money laundering, and fraudulent COVID loan applications.
He agreed to pay $700,000 in restitution and forfeitures, pending up to 50 years of sentencing.

title: The Red Sox have a clear formula for success. Is it sustainable?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox have reached their lowest batting average since 1968 and lowest ERA since 1992 for the current season.
Boston is 14-6 when scoring four or more runs, but struggles at 8-21 with three or fewer runs.
The team has a 3.68 ERA, ranking second in May, but offensive consistency remains a challenge for playoff hopes.

title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter found dangerous, ordered held without bail
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Tyler Brown, accused of Memorial Drive shooting, found dangerous and held without bail after Cambridge District Court hearing.
Brown faces eight charges, including assault to murder, after injuring two people and being shot himself in the incident.
Prosecutors cited Brown's criminal history and a video of the shooting; his lawyers did not contest his detention.

title: Isaiah Thomas hopes ‘second act’ with Celtics will help him become a GM
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Isaiah Thomas joins the Celtics as a scout, aiming to eventually become a team executive.
Thomas will focus on scouting in the West Coast region while residing in Seattle.
Thomas ended his playing career in 2024 and hopes to influence small guard scouting.

title: Woman pleads not guilty in teen’s 2025 drowning in N.H.’s Lake Winnipesaukee
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Buffy Ann Doyle, 50, pleaded not guilty to charges related to a teen's drowning in Lake Winnipesaukee in June 2025.
Doyle is charged with felony negligent homicide and accused of giving the 17-year-old alcohol before the incident.
She allegedly tampered with evidence by discarding alcohol and waived her arraignment, with the next court date set for June 26.

title: Cumberland Farms makes a splash with ‘$99’ coffee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cumberland Farms mistakenly advertised coffee for $99, causing online speculation about inflation and potential AI involvement.
The ad featured peculiar details, sparking debate over whether AI-generated anomalies were responsible for its inaccuracies.
Cumberland Farms, based in Westborough, MA, operates over 1,500 stores in the U.S.; they haven't clarified the ad's intent.

title: Boston City Council votes to keep recent pay raises
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council voted to retain pay increases raising councilor salaries to $125,000 and Mayor Michelle Wu's to $250,000.
Councilors Erin Murphy and Ed Flynn proposed rescinding raises to redirect funds towards services amid budget cuts.
Councilors argued over salary necessity for affordability, referencing past council corruption and cost of living in Boston.

title: HBO documentary series is scouting Cambridge for ‘unique’ neighbor stories
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: HBO's 'Neighbors' documentary is casting for its second season, focusing on unique neighbor stories in Cambridge.
The show's producers are interested in disputes or relationships involving noise, property lines, pets, parking, and construction.
Cambridge residents with notable neighbor stories can submit to the show's casting website for potential inclusion.
